nodes.name: Gradient Oracle Evaluation
nodes.description: Computes objective value and gradient for the current parameters using the provided AD mode, while threading RNG and algorithm state as explicit immutable inputs/outputs.

inputs.name: rng_in
inputs.constraints: Explicit stochastic state input; do not treat as global mutable state.

inputs.name: restructure
inputs.constraints: Pure mapping between flat and structured parameter forms.

outputs.name: state_out
outputs.constraints: Returned explicitly as new state object.

outputs.name: rng_out
outputs.constraints: Returned explicitly (possibly unchanged) to preserve purity.

nodes.type_signature: (rng_in: RNG state / PRNGKey, obj: Objective/model handle, adtype: Automatic differentiation mode/config, out_in: Gradient output container, state_in: Algorithm state, params: Parameter vector/structure, restructure: Parameter restructuring function) -> tuple[Gradient output container, Objective value, Algorithm state, RNG state / PRNGKey]
nodes.conceptual_summary: State-Threaded First-Order Differential Oracle. Evaluates a scalar-valued functional at a candidate point in parameter space and computes its first-order derivative with respect to that point using a selected differentiation strategy. The operation maps between flat and structured parameter representations, writes derivatives into a provided output container, and returns all side-channel context (stochastic key and algorithm state) as explicit immutable outputs. This yields a purity-preserving oracle step suitable for composable iterative methods.. Applications: Calibrating constitutive parameters in computational mechanics by evaluating loss and sensitivity of simulation outputs to model coefficients., Estimating control-policy parameters in robotics using gradient-based updates with explicit simulator and randomness state threading., Fitting inverse-problem parameters in medical imaging reconstruction where objective value and gradient are required per iteration., Optimizing hyperparameters of physics-informed neural operators by computing scalar objective and parameter sensitivities in a reproducible pipeline.
